2605.13484.title: Discovery of Hidden Miscalibration Regimes
2605.13484.paper_summary: Calibration is commonly evaluated by comparing model confidence with its empirical correctness, implicitly treating reliability as a function of the confidence score alone. However, this view can hide substantial structure: models may be systematically overconfident on some kinds of inputs and underconfident on others, causing global reliability diagnostics to obscure localised calibration failures. To address this, we formulate the problem of discovering hidden miscalibration regimes without assuming access to predefined data slices. We define the corresponding miscalibration field and propose a diagnostic framework for estimating it. Our approach learns a calibration-aware representation of the input space and estimates signed local miscalibration by kernel smoothing in the learned geometry. Across four real-world LLM benchmarks and twelve LLMs, we find that input-dependent calibration heterogeneity is prevalent. We further show that the discovered fields are actionable: they support local confidence correction and reduce calibration error in systematically miscalibrated regions where confidence-based methods such as isotonic regression and temperature scaling are less effective.
2605.13484.paper_summary_zh: 校準通常是通過比較模型的信心與其經驗正確性來評估的，隱含地將可靠性視為僅僅依賴信心分數的函數。<lb/>然而，這種觀點可能隱藏了實質性的結構：模型在某些類型的輸入上可能系統性地過度自信，而在其他類型上則自信不足，導致全局可靠性診斷掩蓋了局部的校準失敗。<lb/>為了解決這個問題，我們提出了在不假設訪問預定義數據切片的情況下發現隱藏的錯誤校準狀態的問題。<lb/>我們定義了相應的錯誤校準場並提出了一個估計它的診斷框架。<lb/>我們的方法學習了一個考慮校準的輸入空間表示，並通過在學習的幾何中進行核平滑來估計帶符號的局部錯誤校準。<lb/>在四個現實世界的 LLM 基準和十二個 LLM 中，我們發現輸入依賴的校準異質性是普遍存在的。<lb/>我們進一步表明，發現的場是可操作的：它們支持局部信心修正並減少在系統性錯誤校準區域的校準錯誤，這些區域中基於信心的方法如等距回歸和溫度縮放的效果較差。
2605.13484.author: Katarzyna Kobalczyk et.al.
2605.13484.authors: Katarzyna Kobalczyk, Mihaela van der Schaar
